steps.title: Reverse the prediction
steps.content: The student's hypothesis predicts that the acidic mixture with coffee grounds will be more beneficial for choy sum growth than neutral potting soil. A weakening finding must show the control group doing better under the same growing conditions. That directly reverses the expected benefit of the acidic treatment.

steps.title: Reject supporting results
steps.content: B says the neutral-soil plants weighed less, C says they sprouted later, and D says fewer of them sprouted; each would favor the acidic mixture. The only weakening result is stronger growth in soil without coffee grounds. The answer is A.
